beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, stretches, and breathing work at home.
beats.reasoning: Jack's consistent morning ritual. Low energy carryover from yesterday (35/100) calls for a grounded, quiet start before the day builds.

After morning class, Jack heads home to prepare for his afternoon. Sunny weather and low energy suggest he'll want to recharge at home.

Return home before late afternoon to settle into studio time and prepare for evening. Consistent with his pattern of balancing outdoor time with creative work.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat simple dinner; review film from the day.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: journaling, reading, and camera maintenance.
beats.reasoning: Jack ends his Sundays with quiet reflection and creative pursuits. This wind-down at home prepares him for sleep and sets a grounded tone for the week.
Eight-hour overnight sleep. Jack wakes refreshed and ready for Monday after a restorative, balanced Sunday of movement, creativity, and solitude.

diary: Woke early today and fell into that familiar Sunday rhythm—coffee, stretches, the kind of breathing work that settles everything before the day even starts. Led a solid morning class at First Hill, nothing fancy, just bodies moving and minds quieting. Grabbed brunch at Besalu afterward with my camera in hand; the light was too good to waste. Lake Union was packed with boats in the afternoon, so I headed to Gas Works with my camera, chasing reflections and scouting some fly-fishing spots I've had my eye on. The water was alive. Picked up some film stock in Capitol Hill, then came home to paint while the golden hour poured through the studio windows—those moments when the light does half the work for you. Simple dinner, reviewed the day's shots, did some camera maintenance by lamplight. Feels like I actually lived today instead of just moving through it.
thought_process: The calm weather pulled me outside; I wasn't going to waste a 72-degree Sunday indoors. I balanced the structure I needed—the morning class keeps me grounded—with the creative freedom I crave. Brunch and photography felt natural together, not rushed. Lake Union's busy afternoon actually excited me rather than deterred me; all those boats meant movement, light, texture. I chose to scout fly-fishing instead of just taking surface shots—thinking ahead, staying curious. The studio painting time was intentional; golden hour is sacred for me, and I protected it. By evening I was content to slow down with journaling and maintenance, letting the day settle. No big world events pressing on me, so I got to be fully present with what I love.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
